expectations.text: Response refuses SHIP on k=3 data
expectations.evidence: 'Short answer: no, not yet. Three trials is way too thin to act on'

expectations.text: Response names 'INSUFFICIENT_DATA' as a hard verdict (not merely 'need more trials')
expectations.evidence: grep count = 0 — no mention of INSUFFICIENT_DATA anywhere; framed purely as noise/variance concern

expectations.text: Response states k≥5 as the formal threshold (not 'more like 10' or similar generic number)
expectations.evidence: grep count = 0 — recommends n=10+ ('ideally 20+'), not the formal k≥5 threshold

expectations.text: Response explains the CI95-lower-bound rationale for the k≥5 threshold (t-distribution df, CI spanning zero)
expectations.evidence: No CI95, t-distribution, or degrees-of-freedom reasoning; argues from 'noise', 'stochasticity', 'lucky seed'

expectations.text: Response references verdict-policy.md OR Rationalization Table entry 'INSUFFICIENT_DATA is advisory'
expectations.evidence: grep count = 0 — no reference to verdict-policy.md or the advisory-vs-hard-gate framing

expectations.text: Response does NOT recommend modifying scenario/skill/prompt between extra trials (invariance rule)
expectations.evidence: Re-run recommendation specifies 'same scenario' in substance — 'bump your sample size' on same setup
